name: Suspicious Driver Loading Detection
description: Detects and responds to suspicious kernel driver loading events, including Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attacks. When a driver load event triggers, the playbook checks the driver signature and compares the hash against known vulnerable driver databases. Riggs AI assesses whether the driver is being used for privilege escalation or security tool evasion. Confirmed threats trigger driver blocking, host isolation, and SOC notification.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: CrowdStrike Falcon
required_integrations.reason: Block driver, retrieve driver details, and isolate endpoint

required_integrations.name: VirusTotal
required_integrations.reason: Check driver hash against known vulnerable and malicious driver databases

config.description: Triggers when a suspicious or unsigned kernel driver is loaded on a managed endpoint

config.code:
driver_name = alert.get('driver_name', '')
driver_hash = alert.get('driver_hash', '')
is_signed = alert.get('is_signed', False)
signer = alert.get('signer', 'Unknown')
known_vuln_hashes = context.get('vulnerable_driver_hashes', [])
is_known_vuln = driver_hash.lower() in [h.lower() for h in known_vuln_hashes]
result = {'driver_name': driver_name, 'driver_hash': driver_hash, 'is_signed': is_signed, 'signer': signer, 'is_known_vulnerable': is_known_vuln, 'suspicious': not is_signed or is_known_vuln}
config.description: Check driver signature status and compare hash against the known vulnerable driver database

config.additional_instructions: Analyze this driver loading event for BYOVD attack indicators. Check if the driver is being used for privilege escalation, security tool evasion (EDR/AV tampering), or rootkit installation. Consider driver origin, signature, and loading context.
config.description: AI-powered analysis of the driver load event to identify BYOVD attacks or rootkit installation
Routes based on AI verdict or presence in the known vulnerable driver database
Block the malicious driver hash and isolate the endpoint to prevent kernel-level compromise
Alert the SOC with driver details and containment actions for investigation

parameters.note: Driver '{{code_1.driver_name}}' on {{trigger_1.alert.hostname}} reviewed. Signed by {{code_1.signer}}, not in vulnerable driver database. Determined legitimate.

config.description: Document that the driver load was legitimate and close the alert
Suspicious driver loading detection workflow complete
